words.explanation: 'coger' is normal in Spain for catching transport; avoid it in parts of Latin America.

sentences.text: El conductor dice que la parada es la siguiente.
sentences.translation: The driver says the stop is the next one.

sentences.text: ¿Hay un asiento libre al lado de la ventana?
sentences.translation: Is there a free seat next to the window?

sentences.text: El trayecto dura media hora más o menos.
sentences.translation: The journey lasts about half an hour.

sentences.text: ¡Corre! Vamos a perder el tren.
sentences.translation: Run! We're going to miss the train.

chunks.meaning: to miss the train, miss the boat
chunks.explanation: Literal (miss the train) and figurative (miss an opportunity).

sentences.text: Por suerte llegamos justo a tiempo.
sentences.translation: Luckily we arrive just in time.

sentences.text: Uf, menos mal. Casi lo perdemos.
sentences.translation: Phew, thank goodness. We almost missed it.

lessons.title: Can I speak with...?
lessons.description: Make phone calls and arrange appointments politely.

sentences.text: Buenos días, ¿puedo hablar con la doctora Ruiz?
sentences.translation: Good morning, can I speak with Dr. Ruiz?

sentences.text: ¿De parte de quién, por favor?
sentences.translation: Who's calling, please?

chunks.explanation: Standard phone phrase asking who is on the line.

sentences.text: De parte de Carlos García. Quiero pedir una cita.
sentences.translation: It's Carlos García. I'd like to make an appointment.

sentences.text: Un momento, no cuelgue, por favor.
sentences.translation: One moment, don't hang up, please.

sentences.text: Lo siento, la línea está ocupada ahora.
sentences.translation: Sorry, the line is busy right now.

sentences.text: ¿Puede dejar un recado? Sí, claro.
sentences.translation: Can you leave a message? Yes, of course.

sentences.text: Dígale que llamo más tarde a su móvil.
sentences.translation: Tell her I'll call her mobile later.

sentences.text: ¿Está disponible el jueves por la mañana?
sentences.translation: Is she available on Thursday morning?

sentences.text: Es un poco urgente, si es posible.
sentences.translation: It's a bit urgent, if possible.

sentences.text: Perdón, ¿me marca otra vez el número?
sentences.translation: Sorry, could you dial the number again for me?

sentences.text: Perdón, iba a llamar y se me fue el santo al cielo.
sentences.translation: Sorry, I was going to call and it completely slipped my mind.

chunks.surface: se me fue el santo al cielo
chunks.explanation: Literally 'the saint went up to heaven on me'. A vivid way to say you totally forgot.

sentences.text: No pasa nada. Le esperamos el jueves.
sentences.translation: No problem. We'll expect you on Thursday.

lessons.title: For you and for me
lessons.description: The tricky pair por and para, with common set phrases.

sentences.text: Este regalo es para ti.
sentences.translation: This gift is for you.

grammar.explanation: 'para' marks the recipient or destination: para ti, para mi madre.

sentences.text: Gracias por el regalo, es precioso.
sentences.translation: Thanks for the gift, it's lovely.

grammar.explanation: 'por' gives the reason or cause: gracias por, por amor, por el ruido.

sentences.text: Estudio mucho para aprobar el examen.
sentences.translation: I study a lot to pass the exam.

grammar.explanation: 'para' + infinitive expresses purpose: estudio para aprender.

sentences.text: Trabajo para una empresa muy grande.
sentences.translation: I work for a very big company.

sentences.text: Paso por tu casa a las ocho, ¿vale?
sentences.translation: I'll come by your house at eight, okay?

grammar.explanation: 'por' can mean through, along or by a place: paso por el parque.

sentences.text: Cambio este café por un té, por favor.
sentences.translation: I'll swap this coffee for a tea, please.

grammar.explanation: 'por' marks an exchange: cambio esto por eso, pago diez euros por el libro.

sentences.text: Por eso siempre llego tarde al trabajo.
sentences.translation: That's why I'm always late for work.

sentences.text: ¡Por fin es viernes! Estoy feliz.
sentences.translation: Finally it's Friday! I'm happy.

sentences.text: Necesito el informe para el lunes.
sentences.translation: I need the report by Monday.

grammar.explanation: 'para' marks a deadline: para mañana, para las cinco.

sentences.text: Este bolígrafo sirve para escribir bien.
sentences.translation: This pen is good for writing well.

chunks.meaning: is used for, is good for

sentences.text: Llevo el paraguas por si acaso llueve.
sentences.translation: I'm taking the umbrella just in case it rains.

chunks.explanation: A fixed phrase meaning 'just in case'. Can stand alone or be followed by a clause.

sentences.text: Buena idea, más vale prevenir.
sentences.translation: Good idea, better safe than sorry.

chunks.explanation: Short for 'más vale prevenir que curar' (prevention is better than cure).

lessons.title: I have to do it
lessons.description: Express obligation and necessity in different ways.

sentences.text: Tengo que estudiar para el examen de mañana.
sentences.translation: I have to study for tomorrow's exam.

grammar.explanation: 'tener que' + infinitive expresses a personal obligation: tengo que trabajar.

sentences.text: Hay que llegar pronto para coger sitio.
sentences.translation: You have to arrive early to get a spot.

grammar.explanation: 'hay que' + infinitive is a general, impersonal obligation: hay que estudiar.

sentences.text: Debes descansar más, tienes mala cara.
sentences.translation: You should rest more, you look unwell.

grammar.explanation: 'deber' + infinitive gives advice or a soft obligation: debes comer bien.

chunks.explanation: Literally 'you have a bad face'. Said when someone looks ill or tired.

sentences.text: Necesitamos un documento importante para la cita.
sentences.translation: We need an important document for the appointment.

sentences.text: Es urgente. Tenemos que darnos prisa.
sentences.translation: It's urgent. We have to hurry.

sentences.text: Hay que tener cuidado con el tráfico.
sentences.translation: You have to be careful with the traffic.

sentences.text: Debemos respetar las reglas del edificio.
sentences.translation: We must respect the building's rules.

sentences.text: Tienes que ser más responsable con el dinero.
sentences.translation: You have to be more responsible with money.

sentences.text: No hay que preocuparse tanto por todo.
sentences.translation: You shouldn't worry so much about everything.

sentences.text: Tengo que pagar el alquiler antes del día cinco.
sentences.translation: I have to pay the rent before the fifth.

sentences.text: No me gusta madrugar, pero no queda otra.
sentences.translation: I don't like getting up early, but there's no other choice.

chunks.explanation: Short for 'no queda otra opción'. Means you have no alternative.

sentences.text: Ya lo sé. Hay que hacerlo y punto.
sentences.translation: I know. You just have to do it, end of story.

chunks.meaning: and that's that, end of story
chunks.explanation: Added to end a discussion firmly: 'lo hago y punto'.

lessons.title: Always, sometimes, never
lessons.description: Frequency words and habits with soler.

sentences.text: Siempre desayuno café con tostadas.
sentences.translation: I always have coffee and toast for breakfast.

sentences.text: Nunca llego tarde a una reunión.
sentences.translation: I never arrive late to a meeting.

sentences.text: A veces voy al gimnasio por la tarde.
sentences.translation: Sometimes I go to the gym in the afternoon.

sentences.text: Normalmente ceno sobre las nueve.
sentences.translation: I usually have dinner around nine.

sentences.text: Suelo pasear al perro después de comer.
sentences.translation: I usually walk the dog after lunch.

grammar.explanation: 'soler' + infinitive expresses a habit: suelo levantarme temprano.

sentences.text: Voy al cine a menudo, una vez a la semana.
sentences.translation: I go to the cinema often, once a week.

sentences.text: De vez en cuando salimos a bailar.
sentences.translation: Every now and then we go out dancing.

chunks.explanation: A common way to say 'occasionally, from time to time'.

sentences.text: Casi nunca veo la televisión.
sentences.translation: I hardly ever watch television.

sentences.text: Es una costumbre nuestra, cada domingo.
sentences.translation: It's a habit of ours, every Sunday.

sentences.text: Mi rutina es siempre la misma, un poco aburrida.
sentences.translation: My routine is always the same, a bit boring.

sentences.text: ¿Cada cuánto ves a tus abuelos?
sentences.translation: How often do you see your grandparents?

sentences.text: Los veo de higos a brevas, por desgracia.
sentences.translation: I see them once in a blue moon, unfortunately.

chunks.meaning: once in a blue moon, very rarely
chunks.explanation: Literally 'from figs to early figs', referring to the long gap between harvests. Means very rarely.

lessons.title: A day out in the city
lessons.description: A1 review: plans, directions and requests all together.

sentences.text: ¿Qué te apetece hacer hoy? Podemos hacer turismo.
sentences.translation: What do you fancy doing today? We can do some sightseeing.

sentences.text: Primero vamos a pasear por el casco antiguo.
sentences.translation: First we're going to stroll through the old town.

sentences.text: Necesitamos un mapa. ¿Lo tienes tú?
sentences.translation: We need a map. Do you have it?

words.explanation: Ends in -a but is masculine: el mapa.

sentences.text: Sí, y también tengo la guía del museo.
sentences.translation: Yes, and I also have the museum guide.

sentences.text: Perdone, ¿el museo está lejos de aquí?
sentences.translation: Excuse me, is the museum far from here?

sentences.text: Está cerca. Siga recto y gire a la derecha.
sentences.translation: It's close. Go straight and turn right.

sentences.text: Luego podemos tomar algo y sacar fotos.
sentences.translation: Then we can have a drink and take photos.

sentences.text: Al final vamos a comprar un recuerdo bonito.
sentences.translation: At the end we're going to buy a nice souvenir.

sentences.text: Estoy un poco cansada, pero muy contenta.
sentences.translation: I'm a bit tired, but very happy.

sentences.text: Ha sido un día muy divertido, ¿verdad?
sentences.translation: It's been a really fun day, hasn't it?

sentences.text: Sí, lo hemos pasado bomba. ¡Qué bien!
sentences.translation: Yes, we've had a blast. How great!

chunks.meaning: we had a blast, a great time
chunks.explanation: From 'pasarlo bomba', very colloquial for having a fantastic time.

sentences.text: Vale la pena repetir otro día.
sentences.translation: It's worth doing again another day.

chunks.explanation: 'valer la pena' = to be worthwhile: vale la pena verlo.

lessons.title: I've already done it
lessons.description: Talk about recent actions with the present perfect.

sentences.text: Hoy he trabajado mucho en la oficina.
sentences.translation: Today I've worked a lot at the office.

grammar.explanation: Form: haber (he/has/ha/hemos/han) + past participle (-ado/-ido). Used for today and recent past.

sentences.text: ¿Has comido ya? Sí, ya he comido.
sentences.translation: Have you eaten yet? Yes, I've already eaten.

grammar.explanation: 'ya' with the perfect means 'already': ya lo he hecho.

sentences.text: Todavía no he terminado el informe.
sentences.translation: I haven't finished the report yet.

sentences.text: Esta semana hemos empezado un curso nuevo.
sentences.translation: This week we've started a new course.

sentences.text: ¿Alguna vez has probado la paella?
sentences.translation: Have you ever tried paella?

sentences.text: No, nunca la he probado, pero tiene buena pinta.
sentences.translation: No, I've never tried it, but it looks good.

chunks.explanation: 'tener buena pinta' = to look good, especially food or plans.

sentences.text: Mis amigos han llegado hace un momento.
sentences.translation: My friends have arrived a moment ago.

sentences.text: ¿Qué ha pasado? Pareces preocupado.
sentences.translation: What's happened? You look worried.

sentences.text: He perdido el móvil. No lo encuentro.
sentences.translation: I've lost my phone. I can't find it.

sentences.text: He hecho todo lo posible, pero nada.
sentences.translation: I've done everything possible, but nothing.

words.explanation: Irregular participle: hacer → hecho.

sentences.text: ¡Ya era hora de que llegaras!
sentences.translation: About time you got here!

chunks.explanation: Said when something long-awaited finally happens: '¡Ya era hora!'

sentences.text: Perdona, he tenido un día horrible.
sentences.translation: Sorry, I've had a horrible day.

lessons.title: Yesterday I worked
lessons.description: The preterite of regular -ar verbs for completed past actions.

sentences.text: Ayer trabajé hasta muy tarde.
sentences.translation: Yesterday I worked until very late.

grammar.explanation: Regular -ar preterite: trabajé, trabajaste, trabajó, trabajamos, trabajaron.

sentences.text: ¿Estudiaste anoche para el examen?
sentences.translation: Did you study last night for the exam?

sentences.text: La semana pasada visitamos a mis abuelos.
sentences.translation: Last week we visited my grandparents.

sentences.text: Ana compró un vestido precioso ayer.
sentences.translation: Ana bought a lovely dress yesterday.

sentences.text: Anoche cociné una cena especial para todos.
sentences.translation: Last night I cooked a special dinner for everyone.

sentences.text: ¿A qué hora terminaste ayer?
sentences.translation: What time did you finish yesterday?

sentences.text: Empecé a las ocho y acabé a las seis.
sentences.translation: I started at eight and finished at six.

words.explanation: Spelling change: empezar → empecé (z becomes c before e).

sentences.text: Mis amigos llegaron tarde a la fiesta.
sentences.translation: My friends arrived late to the party.

words.explanation: Spelling change: llegar → llegué (yo), but llegaron is regular.

sentences.text: El sábado pasado descansamos todo el día.
sentences.translation: Last Saturday we rested all day.

sentences.text: ¿Con quién hablaste por teléfono?
sentences.translation: Who did you talk to on the phone?

sentences.text: Fue una relación difícil, pero pasé página.
sentences.translation: It was a difficult relationship, but I moved on.

chunks.meaning: I turned the page, moved on
chunks.explanation: 'pasar página' = to move on, put the past behind you.

sentences.text: Hiciste bien. La vida sigue.
sentences.translation: You did the right thing. Life goes on.

lessons.title: I ate and I left
lessons.description: The preterite of regular -er and -ir verbs.

sentences.text: Anoche comí en un restaurante nuevo.
sentences.translation: Last night I ate at a new restaurant.

grammar.explanation: Regular -er/-ir preterite: comí, comiste, comió, comimos, comieron.

sentences.text: Primero pedí una sopa y después bebí un vino.
sentences.translation: First I ordered a soup and then I drank a wine.

sentences.text: ¿A qué hora saliste de casa esta mañana?
sentences.translation: What time did you leave home this morning?

sentences.text: Salí temprano y volví muy tarde.
sentences.translation: I left early and came back very late.

sentences.text: Escribí tres correos y recibí una respuesta.
sentences.translation: I wrote three emails and got one reply.

sentences.text: Después de la reunión, decidí ir a casa.
sentences.translation: After the meeting, I decided to go home.

sentences.text: Ana conoció a mi hermano en la fiesta.
sentences.translation: Ana met my brother at the party.

words.explanation: In the preterite, 'conocer' means 'to meet someone for the first time'.

sentences.text: Aprendí mucho en ese curso de cocina.
sentences.translation: I learned a lot in that cooking course.

sentences.text: Perdimos el autobús y esperamos media hora.
sentences.translation: We missed the bus and waited half an hour.

sentences.text: De repente, empezó a llover muy fuerte.
sentences.translation: Suddenly, it started to rain very hard.

chunks.explanation: A very common connector to introduce a sudden event in a story.

sentences.text: Corrimos a casa y subimos las escaleras.
sentences.translation: We ran home and went up the stairs.

sentences.text: Al final, todo salió bien.
sentences.translation: In the end, everything turned out fine.

lessons.title: I went and I did it
lessons.description: The most common irregular preterites: ir, ser, hacer, dar and ver.

sentences.text: Ayer fui al médico por la mañana.
sentences.translation: Yesterday I went to the doctor in the morning.

grammar.explanation: ir and ser share the same preterite: fui, fuiste, fue, fuimos, fueron. Context tells them apart.

sentences.text: El viaje fue largo pero muy bonito.
sentences.translation: The trip was long but very nice.

sentences.text: ¿Qué hiciste el fin de semana?
sentences.translation: What did you do at the weekend?

grammar.explanation: hacer: hice, hiciste, hizo, hicimos, hicieron. Note 'hizo' with a z.

sentences.text: Hice una excursión a la montaña con amigos.
sentences.translation: I did a hike in the mountains with friends.

sentences.text: Mi madre hizo una tarta deliciosa.
sentences.translation: My mother made a delicious cake.

sentences.text: Le di un regalo a mi amiga por su cumpleaños.
sentences.translation: I gave my friend a present for her birthday.

grammar.explanation: dar: di, diste, dio, dimos, dieron. ver: vi, viste, vio, vimos, vieron. No accents.

sentences.text: Anoche vimos una película muy buena.
sentences.translation: Last night we saw a very good film.

sentences.text: ¿Viste el partido de fútbol ayer?
sentences.translation: Did you see the football match yesterday?

sentences.text: Fuimos al concierto y lo pasamos genial.
sentences.translation: We went to the concert and had a great time.

sentences.text: Después de discutir, hicimos las paces.
sentences.translation: After arguing, we made up.

chunks.explanation: 'hacer las paces' = to reconcile after a fight.

sentences.text: Ellos fueron muy amables con nosotros.
sentences.translation: They were very kind to us.

sentences.text: Fue un día que nunca voy a olvidar.
sentences.translation: It was a day I'll never forget.

lessons.title: They couldn't come
lessons.description: More irregular preterites: tener, estar, poder, poner, venir and decir.

sentences.text: Ayer tuve un día muy complicado.
sentences.translation: Yesterday I had a very complicated day.

grammar.explanation: These share endings -e/-iste/-o/-imos/-ieron with no accent: tuve, estuve, pude, puse.

sentences.text: Estuvimos en casa de Marta toda la tarde.
sentences.translation: We were at Marta's house all afternoon.

sentences.text: No pude terminar el trabajo a tiempo.
sentences.translation: I couldn't finish the work in time.

sentences.text: Puse las llaves en la mesa y desaparecieron.
sentences.translation: I put the keys on the table and they disappeared.

sentences.text: Mis primos vinieron a visitarnos el domingo.
sentences.translation: My cousins came to visit us on Sunday.

grammar.explanation: venir → vine, viniste, vino; decir → dije, dijiste, dijo. Note 'dijeron', not 'dijieron'.

sentences.text: ¿Por qué no viniste a la reunión?
sentences.translation: Why didn't you come to the meeting?

sentences.text: Me dijo que tuvo un problema con el coche.
sentences.translation: He told me he had a problem with the car.

sentences.text: Le dije la verdad, pero no me creyó.
sentences.translation: I told him the truth, but he didn't believe me.

sentences.text: Cuando lo supo, se enfadó muchísimo.
sentences.translation: When he found out, he got really angry.

words.explanation: In the preterite, 'saber' means 'to find out': supe, supiste, supo.

sentences.text: Puso el grito en el cielo por una tontería.
sentences.translation: He hit the roof over something silly.

chunks.surface: Puso el grito en el cielo
chunks.meaning: hit the roof, made a huge fuss
chunks.explanation: 'poner el grito en el cielo', literally 'to put the shout in the sky'. Means to protest loudly.

sentences.text: Al final no quisieron venir con nosotros.
sentences.translation: In the end they refused to come with us.

words.explanation: In the negative preterite, 'no quiso' often means 'refused'.

sentences.text: Fue una pena, pero no pasó nada grave.
sentences.translation: It was a shame, but nothing serious happened.

lessons.title: When I was a child
lessons.description: The imperfect tense for past habits and descriptions.

sentences.text: Cuando era pequeño, vivía en un pueblo.
sentences.translation: When I was little, I lived in a village.

grammar.explanation: Imperfect for past habits/descriptions: -ar → -aba; -er/-ir → -ía. It sets the scene.

sentences.text: Antes jugaba en el campo todos los días.
sentences.translation: Back then I played in the countryside every day.

sentences.text: De pequeña me gustaba mucho leer cuentos.
sentences.translation: As a little girl I really liked reading stories.

sentences.text: Mi abuelo siempre nos contaba historias.
sentences.translation: My grandfather always told us stories.

sentences.text: Íbamos a la playa cada verano en familia.
sentences.translation: We went to the beach every summer as a family.

words.explanation: 'ir' is irregular in the imperfect: iba, ibas, iba, íbamos, iban.

sentences.text: El colegio estaba cerca de nuestra casa.
sentences.translation: The school was near our house.

sentences.text: Mientras mi madre cocinaba, yo ponía la mesa.
sentences.translation: While my mother cooked, I set the table.

sentences.text: Teníamos un perro que se llamaba Toby.
sentences.translation: We had a dog called Toby.

sentences.text: Nuestro vecino era muy amable con todos.
sentences.translation: Our neighbour was very kind to everyone.

sentences.text: Recuerdo esos días con mucho cariño.
sentences.translation: I remember those days very fondly.

sentences.text: En aquella época todo parecía más sencillo.
sentences.translation: Back in those days everything seemed simpler.

chunks.explanation: A set phrase to introduce memories of a past period.

sentences.text: Sí, la infancia siempre se recuerda mejor.
sentences.translation: Yes, childhood is always remembered better.

lessons.title: It used to be different
lessons.description: Irregular imperfect verbs and talking about how things used to be.

sentences.text: Antes este barrio era muy tranquilo.
sentences.translation: This neighbourhood used to be very quiet.

grammar.explanation: Only three verbs are irregular in the imperfect: ser (era), ir (iba), ver (veía).

sentences.text: De joven, iba al trabajo en bicicleta.
sentences.translation: When I was young, I went to work by bike.

sentences.text: Veía a mis amigos casi todos los días.
sentences.translation: I used to see my friends almost every day.

sentences.text: Solía leer un libro cada semana.
sentences.translation: I used to read a book every week.

grammar.explanation: 'solía' + infinitive is a natural way to say 'used to' do something.

sentences.text: La ciudad era más pequeña y menos moderna.
sentences.translation: The city was smaller and less modern.

sentences.text: La gente se conocía y todos eran vecinos.
sentences.translation: People knew each other and everyone was a neighbour.

sentences.text: Las cosas han cambiado mucho desde entonces.
sentences.translation: Things have changed a lot since then.

sentences.text: Mis abuelos vivían en una casa antigua.
sentences.translation: My grandparents lived in an old house.

sentences.text: En invierno, iba a esquiar con mi familia.
sentences.translation: In winter, I used to go skiing with my family.

sentences.text: Todo era diferente, pero también bonito.
sentences.translation: Everything was different, but also nice.

sentences.text: ¡Cómo pasa el tiempo! Parece que fue ayer.
sentences.translation: How time flies! It feels like yesterday.

chunks.explanation: A nostalgic set phrase, said when reflecting on the past.

sentences.text: Es verdad, los años pasan volando.
sentences.translation: It's true, the years fly by.

chunks.explanation: 'pasar volando', literally 'to pass flying'. Used for time going quickly.

lessons.title: I was cooking when he arrived
lessons.description: Combine the preterite and the imperfect to tell what happened.

sentences.text: Cocinaba tranquilamente cuando sonó el teléfono.
sentences.translation: I was cooking calmly when the phone rang.

grammar.meaning: I was cooking when it rang
grammar.explanation: The imperfect sets the ongoing scene; the preterite marks the interrupting action.

sentences.text: Dormía cuando de repente oí un ruido.
sentences.translation: I was sleeping when suddenly I heard a noise.

sentences.text: Estaba lloviendo cuando salimos de casa.
sentences.translation: It was raining when we left the house.

grammar.explanation: 'estaba' + gerund describes an action in progress in the past.

sentences.text: Mientras yo leía, mi hermano veía la tele.
sentences.translation: While I was reading, my brother was watching TV.

grammar.explanation: Use two imperfects for two actions happening at the same time.

sentences.text: Caminábamos por el parque cuando empezó a nevar.
sentences.translation: We were walking through the park when it started to snow.

sentences.text: El niño se cayó y se rompió el brazo.
sentences.translation: The child fell and broke his arm.

sentences.text: En ese momento no sabía qué hacer.
sentences.translation: At that moment I didn't know what to do.

sentences.text: Todos estaban asustados por el ruido.
sentences.translation: Everyone was scared by the noise.

sentences.text: Llegué justo cuando cerraban la puerta.
sentences.translation: I arrived just as they were closing the door.

sentences.text: Menos mal que llegamos a tiempo, por los pelos.
sentences.translation: Thank goodness we made it in time, by the skin of our teeth.

chunks.meaning: by the skin of one's teeth, only just
chunks.explanation: Literally 'by the hairs'. Means barely making it in time.

sentences.text: Qué susto. Todo pasó muy rápido.
sentences.translation: What a fright. It all happened very fast.

sentences.text: Al final, todo quedó tranquilo otra vez.
sentences.translation: In the end, everything was calm again.

lessons.title: Let me tell you what happened
lessons.description: Tell a short anecdote using past tenses together.

sentences.text: Resulta que ayer me pasó una cosa increíble.
sentences.translation: So it turns out something incredible happened to me yesterday.

chunks.explanation: A classic way to start telling a story or explaining something.

sentences.text: Iba andando al trabajo, como siempre.
sentences.translation: I was walking to work, as always.

sentences.text: De repente, vi a un actor famoso en la calle.
sentences.translation: Suddenly, I saw a famous actor in the street.

sentences.text: Al principio no me lo creía, ¡era él!
sentences.translation: At first I couldn't believe it, it was him!

sentences.text: Me acerqué y le pedí una foto.
sentences.translation: I went up to him and asked for a photo.

sentences.text: Él, muy amable, dijo que sí con una sonrisa.
sentences.translation: He, very kindly, said yes with a smile.

sentences.text: Al hacernos la foto, me temblaban las manos.
sentences.translation: As we took the photo, my hands were shaking.

grammar.explanation: 'al' + infinitive means 'when/upon doing something': al llegar, al salir.

sentences.text: Me quedé de piedra, no podía hablar.
sentences.translation: I was stunned, I couldn't speak.

chunks.explanation: 'quedarse de piedra', literally 'to turn to stone'. Means to be shocked or speechless.

sentences.text: Total, que ahora tengo una foto genial.
sentences.translation: Anyway, now I have an amazing photo.

chunks.explanation: Used to wrap up or summarise a story.

sentences.text: Por suerte, la batería del móvil aguantó.
sentences.translation: Luckily, the phone battery held up.

sentences.text: ¡No me lo puedo creer! ¡Qué suerte tienes!
sentences.translation: I can't believe it! How lucky you are!

sentences.text: Fue el mejor día de la semana, sin duda.
sentences.translation: It was the best day of the week, without a doubt.

lessons.title: I'll do it tomorrow
lessons.description: Talk about the future with the simple future tense.

sentences.text: Mañana hablaré con el jefe sobre el proyecto.
sentences.translation: Tomorrow I'll talk to the boss about the project.

grammar.explanation: Add the endings to the whole infinitive: hablaré, hablarás, hablará, hablaremos, hablarán.

sentences.text: El año que viene viajaré por Sudamérica.
sentences.translation: Next year I'll travel around South America.

sentences.text: ¿Vendrás a la cena el viernes?
sentences.translation: Will you come to the dinner on Friday?

grammar.explanation: Some verbs change the stem: venir → vendré, tener → tendré, hacer → haré, poder → podré, salir → saldré.

sentences.text: Tendré que ahorrar mucho para el viaje.
sentences.translation: I'll have to save a lot for the trip.

sentences.text: Haré todo lo posible por conseguirlo.
sentences.translation: I'll do everything possible to achieve it.

sentences.text: Algún día cumpliré mi sueño de ser artista.
sentences.translation: One day I'll fulfil my dream of being an artist.

sentences.text: Te prometo que las cosas mejorarán pronto.
sentences.translation: I promise you things will get better soon.

sentences.text: Seguro que aprobarás el examen sin problema.
sentences.translation: I'm sure you'll pass the exam no problem.

sentences.text: Saldremos temprano para evitar el tráfico.
sentences.translation: We'll leave early to avoid the traffic.

sentences.text: ¿Qué será de nosotros en diez años?
sentences.translation: What will become of us in ten years?

sentences.text: No lo sé. El tiempo lo dirá.
sentences.translation: I don't know. Time will tell.

chunks.explanation: A common saying about waiting to see how things turn out.

sentences.text: Sea como sea, lo afrontaremos juntos.
sentences.translation: Whatever happens, we'll face it together.

chunks.meaning: whatever happens, one way or another
chunks.explanation: A set phrase meaning 'no matter what'.

lessons.title: I'd like a coffee, please
lessons.description: The conditional for polite requests and wishes.

sentences.text: Me gustaría un café con leche, por favor.
sentences.translation: I'd like a coffee with milk, please.

grammar.explanation: Add -ía to the infinitive: gustaría, hablaría, comería. Softer and more polite.

sentences.text: ¿Podría ayudarme con estas maletas?
sentences.translation: Could you help me with these suitcases?

grammar.explanation: Irregular verbs use the same stems as the future: podría, tendría, haría, saldría.

sentences.text: ¿Te importaría cerrar la ventana?
sentences.translation: Would you mind closing the window?

chunks.explanation: A very polite way to ask a favour: '¿te importaría...?'

sentences.text: Yo, en tu lugar, hablaría con ella.
sentences.translation: If I were you, I'd talk to her.

chunks.meaning: if I were you, in your place
chunks.explanation: 'yo en tu lugar' introduces advice: what I'd do in your situation.

sentences.text: Deberías descansar más y preocuparte menos.
sentences.translation: You should rest more and worry less.

grammar.explanation: 'deberías' + infinitive is the standard way to give gentle advice.

sentences.text: Me encantaría viajar contigo algún día.
sentences.translation: I'd love to travel with you someday.

sentences.text: ¿Sería posible cambiar la reserva?
sentences.translation: Would it be possible to change the reservation?

sentences.text: Yo que tú, elegiría el otro color.
sentences.translation: If I were you, I'd choose the other colour.

sentences.text: ¿Te apetecería salir a cenar esta noche?
sentences.translation: Would you fancy going out for dinner tonight?

sentences.text: Sería un placer. Gracias por la invitación.
sentences.translation: It would be a pleasure. Thanks for the invitation.

sentences.text: A decir verdad, preferiría quedarme en casa.
sentences.translation: To tell the truth, I'd rather stay home.

chunks.explanation: A set phrase to introduce a frank opinion.

sentences.text: Lo entiendo. Podríamos vernos otro día.
sentences.translation: I understand. We could see each other another day.

lessons.title: Do it like this
lessons.description: Give instructions with affirmative commands.

sentences.text: Escucha con atención y repite conmigo.
sentences.translation: Listen carefully and repeat with me.

grammar.explanation: For regular tú commands, use the él/ella form: escucha, come, abre.

sentences.text: Ven aquí y siéntate a mi lado.
sentences.translation: Come here and sit down next to me.

grammar.explanation: Eight irregulars: ven, haz, pon, di, sal, ten, ve, sé (venir, hacer, poner, decir, salir, tener, ir, ser).

sentences.text: Haz la tarea antes de salir.
sentences.translation: Do the homework before going out.

sentences.text: Pon la mesa, por favor, vamos a comer.
sentences.translation: Set the table, please, we're going to eat.

sentences.text: Dime la verdad, ¿qué ha pasado?
sentences.translation: Tell me the truth, what's happened?

grammar.explanation: Pronouns attach to affirmative commands: dime, dámelo, siéntate.

sentences.text: Sigue esta receta paso a paso.
sentences.translation: Follow this recipe step by step.

sentences.text: Primero, corta las verduras en trozos pequeños.
sentences.translation: First, cut the vegetables into small pieces.

sentences.text: Espera un momento, ahora te ayudo.
sentences.translation: Wait a moment, I'll help you now.

sentences.text: Señora, pase y tome asiento, por favor.
sentences.translation: Ma'am, come in and take a seat, please.

grammar.explanation: For 'usted', use the subjunctive form: pase, tome, haga, ponga.

sentences.text: Ten cuidado con el escalón al salir.
sentences.translation: Be careful with the step on your way out.

sentences.text: ¡No te preocupes! Todo va a salir bien.
sentences.translation: Don't worry! Everything's going to be fine.

chunks.explanation: An extremely common reassurance. Formal: 'no se preocupe'.

sentences.text: Vale, gracias. Eres muy amable.
sentences.translation: Okay, thanks. You're very kind.

lessons.title: Please don't do that
lessons.description: Tell people what not to do with negative commands.

sentences.text: No corras por el pasillo, es peligroso.
sentences.translation: Don't run down the corridor, it's dangerous.

grammar.explanation: Negative tú commands use the subjunctive: no corras, no hables, no comas.

sentences.text: No olvides las llaves al salir.
sentences.translation: Don't forget the keys on your way out.

sentences.text: No me lo digas ahora, luego hablamos.
sentences.translation: Don't tell me now, we'll talk later.

grammar.explanation: With negatives, pronouns go before the verb: no me lo digas, no lo hagas.

sentences.text: Por favor, no toques eso, está caliente.
sentences.translation: Please don't touch that, it's hot.

sentences.text: No gritéis, el bebé está durmiendo.
sentences.translation: Don't shout, the baby is sleeping.

sentences.text: No fumes aquí, está prohibido.
sentences.translation: Don't smoke here, it's forbidden.

sentences.text: No molestes a tu hermana, déjala tranquila.
sentences.translation: Don't bother your sister, leave her alone.

sentences.text: No tardes mucho, te espero abajo.
sentences.translation: Don't be long, I'll wait for you downstairs.

sentences.text: Señor, no se preocupe, hay tiempo de sobra.
sentences.translation: Sir, don't worry, there's plenty of time.

grammar.explanation: For 'usted', use the subjunctive with the pronoun before: no se preocupe, no lo haga.

sentences.text: Ten paciencia y guarda la calma.
sentences.translation: Be patient and keep calm.

sentences.text: Perdona el retraso. No pasa nada, tranquilo.
sentences.translation: Sorry for the delay. It's fine, don't worry.

chunks.explanation: Extremely common. Reassures that something is not a problem.

sentences.text: Gracias por entenderlo. Eres un cielo.
sentences.translation: Thanks for understanding. You're a sweetheart.

chunks.explanation: Literally 'you're a sky'. A warm compliment for a kind person.

lessons.title: I hope you come
lessons.description: A first look at the present subjunctive with wishes and hopes.

sentences.text: Espero que vengas a mi fiesta.
sentences.translation: I hope you come to my party.

grammar.explanation: After 'espero que', use the subjunctive: vengas, llegues, estudies.

sentences.text: Quiero que descanses este fin de semana.
sentences.translation: I want you to rest this weekend.

grammar.explanation: When you want someone else to do something, use 'que' + subjunctive.

sentences.text: Es importante que estudies un poco cada día.
sentences.translation: It's important that you study a little every day.

grammar.explanation: Impersonal expressions of value take the subjunctive.

sentences.text: Espero que llegues bien a casa.
sentences.translation: I hope you get home safely.

sentences.text: Necesito que me ayudes con una cosa.
sentences.translation: I need you to help me with something.

sentences.text: Ojalá haga buen tiempo mañana.
sentences.translation: I hope the weather's nice tomorrow.

chunks.explanation: From Arabic, meaning 'God willing'. Always followed by the subjunctive.

sentences.text: Es mejor que hablemos las cosas con calma.
sentences.translation: It's better if we talk things over calmly.

sentences.text: Quiero que te cuides mucho, ¿vale?
sentences.translation: I want you to take good care of yourself, okay?

sentences.text: Espero que apruebes todos los exámenes.
sentences.translation: I hope you pass all the exams.

sentences.text: Es normal que estés nervioso antes de empezar.
sentences.translation: It's normal to be nervous before starting.

sentences.text: Gracias por todo. ¡Que te vaya bien!
sentences.translation: Thanks for everything. All the best!

chunks.explanation: A warm farewell wishing someone well. Uses the subjunctive 'vaya'.

sentences.text: Igualmente. Nos vemos pronto.
sentences.translation: You too. See you soon.

lessons.title: I hope so!
lessons.description: Use the subjunctive to express wishes, hopes and emotions.

sentences.text: Ojalá tengas mucha suerte en la entrevista.
sentences.translation: I hope you have lots of luck in the interview.

grammar.explanation: 'ojalá' expresses a strong wish and is always followed by the subjunctive.

sentences.text: Me alegro de que estés mejor.
sentences.translation: I'm glad you're feeling better.

grammar.explanation: Emotions like 'me alegro de que' take the subjunctive.

sentences.text: Es una pena que no puedas venir.
sentences.translation: It's a shame you can't come.

sentences.text: Ojalá todo salga bien en la operación.
sentences.translation: I hope everything goes well in the operation.

sentences.text: Espero que tengas un buen viaje.
sentences.translation: I hope you have a good trip.

sentences.text: Es una lástima que se acabe el verano.
sentences.translation: It's a pity that summer is ending.

sentences.text: Ojalá vaya todo según lo planeado.
sentences.translation: Hopefully everything goes as planned.

sentences.text: Me alegra que hayas venido a verme.
sentences.translation: I'm happy you came to see me.

sentences.text: Espero que te mejores muy pronto.
sentences.translation: I hope you get better very soon.

sentences.text: Tengo un examen difícil. ¡Cruzo los dedos!
sentences.translation: I have a hard exam. Fingers crossed!

chunks.explanation: 'cruzar los dedos' = to cross your fingers for luck, just like in English.

sentences.text: Seguro que sale genial. ¡Ánimo!
sentences.translation: I'm sure it'll go great. Good luck!

chunks.meaning: cheer up!, you can do it!

sentences.text: Gracias, eso espero. ¡Qué nervios!
sentences.translation: Thanks, I hope so. So nerve-racking!

lessons.title: I just got home
lessons.description: Useful verb phrases: acabar de, estar a punto de, seguir and volver a.

sentences.text: Acabo de llegar a casa, estoy agotado.
sentences.translation: I've just got home, I'm exhausted.

grammar.explanation: 'acabar de' + infinitive = to have just done something.

sentences.text: Estoy a punto de salir, ¿me esperas?
sentences.translation: I'm about to leave, will you wait for me?

grammar.explanation: 'estar a punto de' + infinitive = to be about to do something.

sentences.text: Sigo estudiando español después de dos años.
sentences.translation: I'm still studying Spanish after two years.

grammar.explanation: 'seguir' + gerund = to keep/continue doing something.

sentences.text: Vuelvo a llamar más tarde, ahora está ocupada.
sentences.translation: I'll call again later, she's busy now.

grammar.explanation: 'volver a' + infinitive = to do something again.

sentences.text: Deja de quejarte y ponte a trabajar.
sentences.translation: Stop complaining and get to work.

grammar.explanation: 'dejar de' = to stop doing; 'ponerse a' = to start doing.

sentences.text: Llevo media hora esperando el autobús.
sentences.translation: I've been waiting half an hour for the bus.

grammar.meaning: I've been waiting half an hour
grammar.explanation: 'llevar' + time + gerund says how long you've been doing something.

sentences.text: Acabamos de comer, ahora toca descansar.
sentences.translation: We've just eaten, now it's time to rest.

sentences.text: El tren está a punto de salir, ¡corre!
sentences.translation: The train is about to leave, run!

sentences.text: Vuelve a explicármelo, no lo he entendido.
sentences.translation: Explain it to me again, I haven't understood it.

sentences.text: Sigo sin entender esta parte, es rara.
sentences.translation: I still don't understand this part, it's odd.

chunks.explanation: 'seguir sin' + infinitive = to still not do/have something.

sentences.text: Lo terminamos en un abrir y cerrar de ojos.
sentences.translation: We'll finish it in the blink of an eye.

chunks.surface: en un abrir y cerrar de ojos
chunks.meaning: in the blink of an eye, in no time
chunks.explanation: Literally 'in an opening and closing of eyes'. Means very quickly.

sentences.text: Perfecto, así acabamos enseguida.
sentences.translation: Perfect, that way we'll finish right away.

lessons.title: Spanish is spoken here
lessons.description: Impersonal and passive se for signs and general statements.

sentences.text: Aquí se habla español e inglés.
sentences.translation: Spanish and English are spoken here.

grammar.explanation: 'se' + verb makes a general, no-subject statement: se dice, se puede, se vive bien.

sentences.text: En esta tienda se venden muebles baratos.
sentences.translation: This shop sells cheap furniture.

grammar.explanation: With a plural thing, the verb is plural: se vende casa, se venden casas.

sentences.text: Se busca camarero con experiencia.
sentences.translation: Waiter with experience wanted.

sentences.text: Se necesita gente para el nuevo proyecto.
sentences.translation: People are needed for the new project.

sentences.text: Se prohíbe fumar dentro del edificio.
sentences.translation: Smoking is prohibited inside the building.

sentences.text: Se ruega no hacer ruido después de las diez.
sentences.translation: Please do not make noise after ten.

sentences.text: ¿Cómo se dice esta palabra en español?
sentences.translation: How do you say this word in Spanish?

sentences.text: Se tarda unos veinte minutos en llegar.
sentences.translation: It takes about twenty minutes to get there.

sentences.text: En el cartel pone: se alquila piso.
sentences.translation: The sign says: flat for rent.

sentences.text: En España se cena bastante tarde.
sentences.translation: In Spain people have dinner quite late.

sentences.text: Con este olor a pan, se me hace la boca agua.
sentences.translation: With this smell of bread, my mouth waters.

chunks.surface: se me hace la boca agua
chunks.explanation: Literally 'the mouth turns to water for me'. Said about tempting food.

sentences.text: A mí también. ¡Vamos a comprar uno!
sentences.translation: Me too. Let's go buy one!

lessons.title: The person who called
lessons.description: Join ideas with relative clauses using que, donde and lo que.

sentences.text: La chica que llamó ayer es mi prima.
sentences.translation: The girl who called yesterday is my cousin.

grammar.explanation: 'que' works for both people and things: el libro que leo, la chica que llamó.

sentences.text: Este es el restaurante donde nos conocimos.
sentences.translation: This is the restaurant where we met.

grammar.explanation: Use 'donde' to link to a place: la ciudad donde vivo.

sentences.text: No entiendo lo que quieres decir.
sentences.translation: I don't understand what you mean.

grammar.explanation: 'lo que' means 'what' or 'the thing that': lo que digo, lo que pasa.

sentences.text: El chico con quien hablé es muy amable.
sentences.translation: The boy I spoke with is very kind.

grammar.explanation: After a preposition, use 'quien' for people: la persona con quien vivo.

sentences.text: Lo que más me gusta es viajar sin planes.
sentences.translation: What I like most is travelling without plans.

sentences.text: El momento en que llegaste fue perfecto.
sentences.translation: The moment you arrived was perfect.

sentences.text: Ese es el tipo de música que me relaja.
sentences.translation: That's the kind of music that relaxes me.

sentences.text: La razón por la que no vine es una tontería.
sentences.translation: The reason I didn't come is a silly one.

sentences.text: Todo lo que dices tiene mucho sentido.
sentences.translation: Everything you say makes a lot of sense.

sentences.text: El asunto del que hablamos es delicado.
sentences.translation: The matter we talked about is delicate.

sentences.text: Por cierto, ¿sabes algo de Marta?
sentences.translation: By the way, have you heard anything from Marta?

chunks.explanation: Used to introduce a new topic or a sudden thought, like 'by the way'.

sentences.text: Es la persona en quien más confío.
sentences.translation: She's the person I trust most.

lessons.title: Although it's late
lessons.description: Connect ideas smoothly with common discourse connectors.

sentences.text: Aunque es tarde, todavía quiero salir.
sentences.translation: Although it's late, I still want to go out.

grammar.explanation: 'aunque' introduces a contrast, like 'although' or 'even though'.

sentences.text: Estoy cansado; sin embargo, voy a terminar esto.
sentences.translation: I'm tired; however, I'm going to finish this.

sentences.text: Llovía mucho, por eso no fuimos a la playa.
sentences.translation: It was raining a lot, that's why we didn't go to the beach.

sentences.text: No había billetes, así que nos quedamos en casa.
sentences.translation: There were no tickets, so we stayed home.

sentences.text: Es barato y, además, es de buena calidad.
sentences.translation: It's cheap and, what's more, it's good quality.

sentences.text: A mí me gusta el mar, mientras que a él le gusta la montaña.
sentences.translation: I like the sea, whereas he likes the mountains.

chunks.explanation: Marks a contrast between two things, unlike 'mientras' (at the same time).

sentences.text: Llegó tarde; o sea, perdió el principio.
sentences.translation: He arrived late; that is, he missed the beginning.

chunks.explanation: Used to rephrase or clarify what you just said.

sentences.text: No me gusta el frío; en cambio, adoro el verano.
sentences.translation: I don't like the cold; on the other hand, I adore summer.

sentences.text: Primero estudiamos y finalmente lo celebramos.
sentences.translation: First we study and finally we celebrate.

sentences.text: Estaba enfermo; de todos modos, fue a trabajar.
sentences.translation: He was sick; anyway, he went to work.

sentences.text: A fin de cuentas, lo importante es la salud.
sentences.translation: At the end of the day, health is what matters.

chunks.meaning: at the end of the day, after all
chunks.explanation: Introduces a final conclusion after weighing things up.

sentences.text: Tienes toda la razón, en resumen.
sentences.translation: You're absolutely right, in short.

lessons.title: I've travelled a lot
lessons.description: Talk about life experiences using the present perfect.

sentences.text: He viajado por muchos países de Europa.
sentences.translation: I've travelled through many countries in Europe.

grammar.explanation: Use the present perfect for experiences in your life so far, without a specific time.

sentences.text: ¿Has estado alguna vez en Sudamérica?
sentences.translation: Have you ever been to South America?

sentences.text: Nunca he probado la comida japonesa.
sentences.translation: I've never tried Japanese food.

sentences.text: He visto paisajes increíbles en mis viajes.
sentences.translation: I've seen incredible landscapes on my travels.

words.explanation: Irregular participle: ver → visto.

sentences.text: He conocido a gente de muchas culturas.
sentences.translation: I've met people from many cultures.

sentences.text: Jamás he visitado un lugar tan bonito.
sentences.translation: I've never visited such a beautiful place.

sentences.text: Aprender otro idioma ha cambiado mi vida.
sentences.translation: Learning another language has changed my life.

sentences.text: El año pasado viajé a México por primera vez.
sentences.translation: Last year I travelled to Mexico for the first time.

grammar.explanation: With a finished time expression (el año pasado, ayer), use the preterite, not the perfect.

sentences.text: Fue una experiencia realmente inolvidable.
sentences.translation: It was a truly unforgettable experience.

sentences.text: En cada viaje he descubierto algo nuevo.
sentences.translation: On each trip I've discovered something new.

words.explanation: Irregular participle: descubrir → descubierto.

sentences.text: En ese buffet nos pusimos las botas.
sentences.translation: At that buffet we stuffed ourselves.

chunks.meaning: we stuffed ourselves, ate our fill
chunks.explanation: 'ponerse las botas', literally 'to put on one's boots'. Means to eat a lot or do very well out of something.

sentences.text: Espero seguir viajando muchos años más.
sentences.translation: I hope to keep travelling for many more years.

lessons.title: You should rest
lessons.description: Give advice about health and wellbeing.

sentences.text: Deberías dormir más, tienes mala cara.
sentences.translation: You should sleep more, you look off-colour.

grammar.explanation: Both give advice; 'tendrías que' feels a bit stronger than 'deberías'.

sentences.text: Te recomiendo que hagas más ejercicio.
sentences.translation: I recommend that you do more exercise.

grammar.explanation: 'recomendar que' and 'aconsejar que' take the subjunctive.

sentences.text: Si te duele la cabeza, tómate una pastilla.
sentences.translation: If your head hurts, take a pill.

grammar.surface: Si te duele la cabeza, tómate
grammar.explanation: 'si' + present tense can be followed by a command for advice.

sentences.text: Tendrías que cuidarte más y comer mejor.
sentences.translation: You should take better care of yourself and eat better.

sentences.text: El estrés no es bueno para la salud.
sentences.translation: Stress isn't good for your health.

sentences.text: Te aconsejo que descanses este fin de semana.
sentences.translation: I advise you to rest this weekend.

sentences.text: Si sigues así, vas a mejorar muy pronto.
sentences.translation: If you keep it up, you'll get better very soon.

sentences.text: No es nada grave, solo un poco de cansancio.
sentences.translation: It's nothing serious, just a bit of tiredness.

sentences.text: Deberías beber más agua durante el día.
sentences.translation: You should drink more water during the day.

sentences.text: Con una buena dieta te sentirás mejor.
sentences.translation: With a good diet you'll feel better.

sentences.text: Mi abuelo está más sano que una manzana.
sentences.translation: My grandfather is as fit as a fiddle.

chunks.meaning: as fit as a fiddle, in great health
chunks.explanation: Literally 'healthier than an apple'. Said about someone in very good health.

sentences.text: ¡Ojalá yo tenga esa salud a su edad!
sentences.translation: I hope I have that kind of health at his age!

lessons.title: In my opinion
lessons.description: Give opinions and agree or disagree.

sentences.text: Creo que esta es la mejor opción.
sentences.translation: I think this is the best option.

grammar.explanation: Affirmative opinions ('creo que', 'me parece que') take the indicative.

sentences.text: Me parece que tienes toda la razón.
sentences.translation: It seems to me you're absolutely right.

sentences.text: No creo que sea una buena idea.
sentences.translation: I don't think it's a good idea.

grammar.explanation: The negative 'no creo que' expresses doubt, so it takes the subjunctive.

sentences.text: Estoy de acuerdo contigo en eso.
sentences.translation: I agree with you on that.

sentences.text: Pues yo no estoy de acuerdo del todo.
sentences.translation: Well, I don't entirely agree.

sentences.text: Desde mi punto de vista, es un error.
sentences.translation: From my point of view, it's a mistake.

sentences.text: Según él, la película es aburrida.
sentences.translation: According to him, the film is boring.

sentences.text: Tienes razón en parte, pero no del todo.
sentences.translation: You're partly right, but not entirely.

sentences.text: Depende de cómo lo mires, la verdad.
sentences.translation: It depends how you look at it, honestly.

sentences.text: Tal vez tengas razón, no lo había pensado.
sentences.translation: Maybe you're right, I hadn't thought of that.

chunks.explanation: 'tal vez' + subjunctive expresses possibility.

sentences.text: Bueno, cada uno a lo suyo, ¿no?
sentences.translation: Well, to each their own, right?

chunks.explanation: Means everyone should mind their own business or do their own thing.

sentences.text: Exacto. Así todos contentos.
sentences.translation: Exactly. That way everyone's happy.

lessons.title: The job interview
lessons.description: Describe your job, skills and experience in a work setting.

sentences.text: Buenos días, gracias por venir a la entrevista.
sentences.translation: Good morning, thank you for coming to the interview.

sentences.text: Cuénteme un poco sobre su experiencia.
sentences.translation: Tell me a little about your experience.

sentences.text: Llevo cinco años trabajando en ventas.
sentences.translation: I've been working in sales for five years.

grammar.meaning: I've been working for five years
grammar.explanation: 'llevar' + time + gerund expresses how long you've been doing something.

sentences.text: En mi puesto actual dirijo un pequeño equipo.
sentences.translation: In my current position I lead a small team.

sentences.text: Se me da bien trabajar bajo presión.
sentences.translation: I'm good at working under pressure.

chunks.explanation: 'dársele bien a alguien' = to be good at something: se me da bien el inglés.

sentences.text: ¿Por qué quiere trabajar en nuestra empresa?
sentences.translation: Why do you want to work at our company?

sentences.text: Me interesa el proyecto y el buen ambiente.
sentences.translation: I'm interested in the project and the good atmosphere.

sentences.text: ¿Cuál sería su sueldo ideal?
sentences.translation: What would be your ideal salary?

sentences.text: El horario es flexible y hay contrato fijo.
sentences.translation: The schedule is flexible and there's a permanent contract.

sentences.text: Tengo muchas ganas de empezar y aprender.
sentences.translation: I'm really eager to start and learn.

sentences.text: Le llamaremos la próxima semana con la respuesta.
sentences.translation: We'll call you next week with our answer.

sentences.text: Muchas gracias por su tiempo. Ha sido un placer.
sentences.translation: Thank you very much for your time. It's been a pleasure.

lessons.title: See you soon!
lessons.description: A warm A2 recap: past narration, plans and heartfelt goodbyes.

sentences.text: No me puedo creer que ya sea el último día.
sentences.translation: I can't believe it's already the last day.

sentences.text: Estos meses juntos han pasado volando.
sentences.translation: These months together have flown by.

sentences.text: Cuando llegué, no hablaba nada de español.
sentences.translation: When I arrived, I didn't speak any Spanish.

grammar.meaning: when I arrived, I didn't speak
grammar.explanation: The preterite 'llegué' marks the event; the imperfect 'hablaba' describes the situation then.

sentences.text: Ahora puedo mantener una conversación entera.
sentences.translation: Now I can hold a whole conversation.

sentences.text: El año que viene volveré para visitaros.
sentences.translation: Next year I'll come back to visit you all.

sentences.text: Espero que mantengamos el contacto siempre.
sentences.translation: I hope we always keep in touch.

sentences.text: Os voy a echar mucho de menos a todos.
sentences.translation: I'm going to miss you all a lot.

chunks.explanation: 'echar de menos' = to miss someone or something.

sentences.text: Nosotros también. Prométenos que vuelves.
sentences.translation: Us too. Promise us you'll come back.

sentences.text: Os deseo lo mejor. Cuidaos mucho, ¿vale?
sentences.translation: I wish you all the best. Take good care, okay?

sentences.text: Gracias por todo. Ha sido una experiencia preciosa.
sentences.translation: Thanks for everything. It's been a beautiful experience.

sentences.text: ¡Buen viaje! Un abrazo muy fuerte.
sentences.translation: Have a good trip! A big hug.

chunks.explanation: A warm way to say goodbye, also common at the end of messages.

sentences.text: Adiós, amigos. ¡Hasta la vista!
sentences.translation: Goodbye, friends. See you around!

chunks.meaning: see you around!, until we meet again
chunks.explanation: A slightly dramatic but common farewell, literally 'until the sight'.
